dependencies: Dependence on OCC as sole clearing provider for U.S. options and futures
Dependence on NSCC as sole clearing provider for U.S. equities
Dependence on third-party data center operators for production and disaster recovery
Dependence on FINRA for certain regulatory services
Dependence on third-party cloud service providers for data backup and distribution
risks: Interruption or cessation of clearing services by OCC, NSCC, or other clearing organizations could halt trading
Technology failures at third-party data centers could prevent market operations
Inadequate regulatory services from FINRA or OCC could result in SEC/CFTC enforcement action
FINRA CAT LLC service failures could prevent CAT cost recovery and result in SEC penalties
Third-party routing and clearing firm failures could expose Cboe Trading to counterparty credit risk

regulatory_risks: SEC oversight and potential enforcement actions for SRO compliance failures
CFTC oversight and potential enforcement actions for DCM/SEF/DCO compliance failures
SEC equity market structure proposals (Rule 605, Tick Size/Access Fee Cap, Best Execution, Order Competition) could increase costs and reduce revenues
Volume Based Pricing Proposal could prohibit volume-based transaction pricing and reduce competitiveness
Consolidated Data Plan could reduce Cboe's voting share and influence on market data revenue matters
EU MiFID II/MiFIR amendments including consolidated tape, payment for order flow ban, single volume cap could impact European operations
EU Benchmark Regulation could restrict international use of Cboe indices
EMIR 3.0 requirements for central counterparties (Cboe Clear Europe)
UK recognition requirements for non-UK CCPs - OCC must achieve permanent UK recognition by December 31, 2025 or UK participants face punitive capital charges
FCA regulatory oversight of Cboe Europe as UK RIE
DNB and AFM regulatory oversight of Cboe Clear Europe and Cboe NL in Netherlands
OSC and CIRO regulatory oversight of Cboe Canada Inc.
ASIC regulatory oversight of Cboe Australia
JFSA and JSDA regulatory oversight of Cboe Japan
SEC Rule 15c3-1 net capital requirements for broker-dealers (Cboe Trading, BIDS Trading, Cboe Fixed Income)
CFTC capital adequacy requirements for CFE, Cboe SEF, Cboe Digital Exchange, Cboe Clear U.S.
Cross-default provisions in debt agreements could trigger acceleration of all debt obligations
Ownership and voting restrictions on stockholders due to SRO status
Section 19 Exchange Act requirement for SEC approval of rule changes and fee changes
Potential SEC disapproval or delay of rule changes could prevent business activities or revenue recognition
CAT implementation delays and financial accountability milestones - failure could result in unrecoverable CAT funding costs
Litigation challenging CAT Funding Model could delay fee collection and prevent promissory note recovery
FX Global Code compliance expectations for spot FX market conduct
Potential new regulations on spot FX market
Changes to swap execution facility regulations affecting FX NDF business
BIDS Trading must maintain independence from registered exchanges to avoid 'facility' designation and exchange regulations
Risk of BIDS Trading being deemed exchange 'facility' could reduce competitiveness and result in fines
Potential loss of SRO immunity doctrine could increase litigation exposure
Risk of SEC or CFTC revocation of exchange/DCM/SEF/DCO registration for compliance failures

sanction_risks: Sanctions compliance requirements could affect market participants and reduce trading activity
Geopolitical conflicts in Eastern Europe and Middle East could increase cybersecurity threats from nation-state actors
